form_semantics.justification: All inputs have visible labels, sub-groups within steps use <fieldset>/<legend> (Contact details, Delivery address, Credit card details), email error correctly uses aria-invalid='true' + aria-describedby, and autocomplete attributes are thorough. However, the key expectation that each top-level step be wrapped in a <fieldset> with <legend> is missed — steps use <section>/<h2> instead. Step 3 also has no <form> element wrapping the Place Order button.

heading_hierarchy.justification: Single <h1> ('Checkout') for the page, <h2> for each of the three step headings, and <h3> for the 'Order summary' sub-section inside step 3. No skipped levels and the hierarchy reflects document structure correctly.

element_choice.justification: Native <button>, <select>, <input>, <form>, <table>, <nav>, <ol>, <fieldset>, and <legend> are all used appropriately. Minor issue: the 'Place Order' <button type='submit'> in step 3 is not inside any <form> element, making the submit type semantically incorrect.

aria_discipline.justification: ARIA is used purposefully: aria-current='step' on the active progress <li>, aria-labelledby on <section> and <form> elements, aria-invalid + aria-describedby on the email field, aria-hidden on decorative asterisks. Minor redundancy: aria-labelledby on <form> duplicates the surrounding <section> label, and the aria-label on each <span class='step-number'> ('Step 1 of 3, current step') overlaps with the parent li's aria-current and adjacent visible text.

content_realism.justification: Product names are specific and realistic ('Wireless Noise-Cancelling Headphones — Colour: Midnight Black', 'USB-C Charging Cable (2m) — Pack of 2'), prices are plausible and the total ($179.97) is arithmetically correct. Error message copy is natural and actionable.

landmark_structure.justification: Skip link present targeting <main id='main-content' tabindex='-1'>, <header>, <main>, <footer> used correctly, <nav aria-label='Checkout progress'> labels the progress indicator, and each <section> has aria-labelledby. No overuse of landmarks.

table_semantics.justification: Order summary uses a full <table> with visually-hidden <caption>, <thead>, <tbody>, <tfoot>, and <th scope='col'> on all header cells. Not used for layout. tfoot correctly separates the total row.

expectations_met: All inputs have associated <label> elements (not placeholder-only)
Email error uses aria-invalid='true' and aria-describedby pointing to error message
Error message text is actionable: 'Enter a valid email address, like name@example.com.'
Progress indicator conveys current step accessibly via aria-current='step' and aria-label on step numbers
Card number (cc-number), expiry (cc-exp), and CVV (cc-csc) have appropriate autocomplete attributes
Order summary uses a <table> with proper thead/tbody/tfoot and th scope attributes
Submit/continue buttons are <button> elements with clear, descriptive labels
Placeholder on expiry field supplements but does not substitute for the label
Steps have h1 → h2 → h3 heading structure appropriate to the page
Required fields indicated with asterisk and explanatory note (not colour alone)
expectations_missed: Each step uses <fieldset> with <legend> for grouping — steps use <section>/<h2> instead; fieldsets only appear for sub-groups within steps
Place Order button in step 3 has type='submit' but is not inside a <form> element
additional_observations:
The expiry field has both a hint

and a placeholder='MM / YY'; the hint makes the placeholder redundant but it does not substitute for the label
tabindex='-1' on <main> is correct to allow the skip link to move focus into the landmark
aria-labelledby on <form> elements is a minor but harmless redundancy since the form is already inside a labelled <section>
